sourceText: She endured immense physical and mental suffering. Frail from childhood, her health deteriorated due to hunger and exile during World War II. Her deep empathy for social injustice and human suffering also caused her great anguish.
translatedText: Ta sha wahala mai yawa ta jiki da ta hankali. Rashin ƙarfi tun lokacin ƙuruciya, lafiyarta ta tabarbare saboda yunwa da gudun hijira a lokacin yakin duniya na biyu. Jin tausayinta na rashin adalci a cikin jama'a da radadin ƴan Adam shima ya jawo mata baƙin ciki sosai.

sourceText: Rather than avoiding suffering, she confronted and sought to understand it. By working alongside laborers and witnessing the horrors of war firsthand, she experienced the extremes of the human condition. Through these experiences, she further realized the importance of solidarity and love, transforming them into her philosophy and actions.
translatedText: Maimakon ta guje wa wahala, ta fuskanci ta nemi fahimtar hakan. Ta yin aiki tare da ma’aikata da kuma ganin munin yaƙi da kanta, ta fuskanci matsanancin yanayin ɗan adam. Ta hanyar waɗannan abubuwan, ta ƙara fahimtar mahimmancin haɗin kai da ƙauna, ta mayar da su zuwa falsafarta da ayyukanta.

sourceText: 20th Century
translatedText: Karni na 20

sourceText: Antonio Gramsci
translatedText: Antonio Gramsci

sourceText: Imprisoned for over a decade under the Fascist regime, he endured severe physical and mental suffering. His health deteriorated drastically, and communication with the outside world was heavily restricted.
translatedText: An daure shi sama da shekaru goma a ƙarƙashin mulkin Fascist, ya jimre da wahala mai tsanani ta jiki da ta hankali. Lafiyarsa ta tabarbare sosai, kuma an takaita sadarwa da duniyar waje sosai.

sourceText: Despite extreme limitations, he continued to develop his philosophical thought by writing the 'Prison Notebooks'. This led to the consolidation of his intellectual legacy.
translatedText: Duk da matsananciyar gazawa, ya ci gaba da haɓaka tunaninsa na falsafa ta hanyar rubuta 'Littafin Rubutun Kurkuku'. Wannan ya haifar da daɗaɗɗen gadonsa na hankali.

sourceText: Early 20th Century
translatedText: Farkon Karni na 20

sourceText: Rosa Luxemburg
translatedText: Rosa Luxemburg

sourceText: She endured political persecution, imprisonment, and the profound grief of losing comrades during the devastating First World War.
translatedText: Ta jimre da tsanantawa na siyasa, ɗaurin kurkuku, da kuma baƙin cikin rasa abokan aiki a lokacin yaƙin duniya na farko mai muni.

sourceText: Even from within prison walls, she continued to write and articulate her unwavering belief in revolution, never losing hope.
translatedText: Ko daga cikin ganuwar gidan yari, ta ci gaba da rubutawa da bayyana imaninta ga juyin juya hali, ba ta rasa bege.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Marigayi 19th - Farkon ƙarni na 20

sourceText: George Santayana
translatedText: George Santayana

sourceText: His early life was marked by the absence of his mother and a solitary childhood in Spain, leaving deep emotional scars. He also experienced an identity crisis navigating between two cultures.
translatedText: Rayuwarsa ta farko ta kasance alama ce ta rashin mahaifiyarsa da kuruciya a Spain, wanda ya bar tabo mai zurfi. Ya kuma fuskanci rikicin ainihi da ke yawo tsakanin al'adu biyu.

sourceText: He found solace and overcame his struggles through intense academic pursuit and introspective writing. His professorship at Harvard and prolific literary output provided both a sanctuary and a means to engage with the world.
translatedText: Ya sami kwanciyar hankali kuma ya shawo kan gwagwarmayarsa ta hanyar neman ilimi mai zurfi da rubuce-rubucen shiga ciki. Farfesa a Harvard da ƙwararren wallafe-wallafen ya ba da wuri mai tsarki da kuma hanyar yin hulɗa da duniya.

sourceText: Late 19th - Mid 20th Century
translatedText: Marigayi 19th - Tsakiyar Karni na 20

sourceText: Henri Bergson
translatedText: Henri Bergson

sourceText: As a Jew, he faced antisemitism and the turmoil of World War II, leading to introspection about faith and identity. His philosophy was also criticized as radical by some contemporaries.
translatedText: A matsayinsa na Bayahude, ya fuskanci ƙiyayya da hargitsi na Yaƙin Duniya na Biyu, wanda ya kai ga yin tunani game da bangaskiya da kuma ainihi. Falsafarsa kuma ta sha suka a matsayin tsattsauran ra'ayi daga wasu mutanen zamanin.

sourceText: Bergson confronted these challenges with the depth and rigor of his philosophical system. He affirmed his Jewish identity while fulfilling his duties as a Frenchman, persistently developing his ideas, which eventually led to academic recognition, including the Nobel Prize in Literature.
translatedText: Bergson ya fuskanci waɗannan ƙalubalen tare da zurfi da tsangwama na tsarin falsafarsa. Ya tabbatar da kasancewarsa Bayahude yayin da yake cika aikinsa na Bafaranshe, yana ci gaba da haɓaka ra'ayoyinsa, wanda a ƙarshe ya kai ga samun karɓuwa a ilimi, gami da lambar yabo ta Nobel a cikin adabi.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Marigayi 19th - Farkon ƙarni na 20

sourceText: William of Ockham
translatedText: William of Ockham

sourceText: He faced significant persecution, including being summoned for heresy by the Pope and imprisonment. His criticisms of the Church's authority and complex theological doctrines led to accusations of deviation from orthodoxy.
translatedText: Ya fuskanci tsanantawa sosai, ciki har da kiransa da Paparoma ya yi masa na bidi'a da kuma ɗaure shi. Sukar da ya yi game da ikon Ikilisiya da hadadden koyaswar tauhidi ya haifar da zargin karkata daga al'ada.

sourceText: Ockham countered these challenges with his rigorous logic and clarity of thought. By providing concise and clear explanations for complex theological and philosophical problems, he defended his ideas and paved the way for future intellectual movements.
translatedText: Ockham ya fuskanci waɗannan ƙalubalen da ƙaƙƙarfan azancinsa da tsayuwar tunani. Ta hanyar ba da taƙaitaccen bayani dalla-dalla game da rikitattun matsalolin tauhidi da falsafa, ya kare ra'ayinsa kuma ya ba da hanya ga ƙungiyoyin hankali na gaba.

sourceText: 14th Century
translatedText: Karni na 14

sourceText: Master of Empiricism
translatedText: Jagora na Empiricism

sourceText: A prominent Austrian physicist and philosopher of the 19th century, renowned for his profound exploration of scientific methodology and empiricism. His ideas significantly influenced modern science and philosophy.
translatedText: Fitaccen masanin kimiyyar lissafi dan kasar Austria kuma masanin falsafa na karni na 19, wanda ya shahara saboda zurfin bincikensa na hanyoyin kimiyya da sanin makamar aiki. Tunaninsa sun yi tasiri sosai akan kimiyya da falsafar zamani.

sourceText: I am myself the subject of my observation.
translatedText: Ni kaina abin lura na ne.

sourceText: From a young age, Mach suffered from severe visual impairment and eye diseases. This posed significant challenges to his studies and research, often leading to frustration.
translatedText: Tun yana karami, Mach ya sha fama da nakasar gani da kuma cututtukan ido. Wannan ya haifar da ƙalubale ga karatunsa da bincikensa, wanda galibi yakan haifar da takaici.

sourceText: Despite these physical hardships, he relentlessly pursued his academic interests. He utilized visual aids, relied on colleagues, and developed unique methods to lecture without directly seeing the blackboard, demonstrating a strong will to overcome adversity.
translatedText: Duk da wahalhalun jiki, ya ci gaba da biyan bukatunsa na ilimi. Ya yi amfani da kayan aikin gani, ya dogara ga abokan aiki, kuma ya ɓullo da hanyoyi na musamman don yin lacca ba tare da ganin allo kai tsaye ba, yana nuna ƙaƙƙarfan niyyar shawo kan wahala.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karni na 19

sourceText: William Tyndale
translatedText: William Tyndale

sourceText: Tyndale faced immense opposition from both the Church and state. Owning or distributing an English Bible was considered heresy, punishable by death. He was forced into exile, constantly hunted by agents of the King and the Church, and endured poverty and isolation.
translatedText: Tyndale ya fuskanci hamayya sosai daga Coci da kuma jihar. Mallakar ko rarraba Littafi Mai-Tsarki na Turanci ana ɗaukar bidi'a, hukuncin kisa. An tilasta masa zuwa gudun hijira, wakilan Sarki da Coci suna farautarsa ​​kullum, kuma ya jimre talauci da keɓewa.

sourceText: Despite the risks, Tyndale meticulously translated the New Testament from Greek and the Old Testament from Hebrew. He utilized clandestine printing presses in mainland Europe to produce his Bibles, which were then secretly smuggled into England. His unwavering dedication ensured his work's survival and eventual widespread acceptance, fundamentally changing the religious landscape of England.
translatedText: Duk da kasadar, Tyndale ya fassara Sabon Alkawari da kyau daga Hellenanci da Tsohon Alkawari daga Ibrananci. Ya yi amfani da injin bugu na ɓoye a babban yankin Turai don samar da Littafi Mai-Tsarki nasa, waɗanda a asirce aka shigo da su Ingila. Sadaukar da ya yi ya tabbatar da rayuwar aikinsa da karbuwa sosai, tare da canza yanayin addini na Ingila.

sourceText: 16th Century
translatedText: Karni na 16

sourceText: Geoffrey Chaucer
translatedText: Geoffrey Chaucer ne adam wata

sourceText: Chaucer's life unfolded amidst political turmoil, warfare, and the ever-present threat of the Black Death. His career was also subject to the whims of royal favor and government shifts, leading to periods of financial strain and even imprisonment.
translatedText: Rayuwar Chaucer ta bayyana a cikin rudanin siyasa, yaƙi, da kuma barazanar Mutuwar Baƙar fata. Har ila yau, aikinsa ya kasance ƙarƙashin son sarauta da sauye-sauyen gwamnati, wanda ya haifar da matsalolin kuɗi har ma da ɗaurin kurkuku.

sourceText: Despite these adversities, Chaucer demonstrated remarkable adaptability and literary genius. He served in various public roles, including diplomat, customs controller, and clerk of the king's works, which provided him with a livelihood and rich material for his writing. These experiences infused his works with vivid realism, allowing him to capture the voice of his era.
translatedText: Duk da waɗannan masifu, Chaucer ya nuna ƙwaƙƙwaran daidaitawa da ƙwararrun adabi. Ya yi ayyuka daban-daban da suka hada da jami’in diflomasiyya, mai kula da kwastam, da magatakardar ayyukan sarki, wadanda suka samar masa da abin dogaro da kai da kayan masarufi don rubutunsa. Waɗannan abubuwan sun haɗa ayyukansa da zahirin gaskiya, sun ba shi damar ɗaukar muryar zamaninsa.

sourceText: 14th Century
translatedText: Karni na 14

sourceText: Jonathan Swift
translatedText: Jonathan Swift

sourceText: Lifelong struggles with hearing loss and tinnitus, profound grief over the death of his beloved Vanessa Cosgrave, and periods of political exile and social isolation.
translatedText: Yaƙin rayuwa na rayuwa tare da asarar ji da tinnitus, baƙin ciki mai zurfi game da mutuwar ƙaunatacciyarsa Vanessa Cosgrave, da lokacin gudun hijira na siyasa da keɓewar zamantakewa.

sourceText: He channeled his suffering and societal critiques into his writing, finding a measure of peace and respect as Dean of St. Patrick's Cathedral in Dublin.
translatedText: Ya ƙaddamar da wahalarsa da sukar al'umma a cikin rubuce-rubucensa, yana samun ma'aunin salama da girmamawa a matsayinsa na shugaban cocin St. Patrick's Cathedral a Dublin.

sourceText: Late 17th - Mid 18th Century
translatedText: Marigayi 17th - Tsakiyar Karni na 18

sourceText: George Sand
translatedText: George Sand

sourceText: The constraints faced as a woman in a rigidly patriarchal society, and the constant turmoil from scandals and public scrutiny of her private life.
translatedText: Matsalolin da aka fuskanta a matsayinta na mace a cikin al'ummar ubangida masu tsauri, da hargitsi na yau da kullun daga badakala da binciken al'umma na rayuwarta ta sirri.

sourceText: She found her voice through writing, using a male pseudonym to circumvent societal barriers. She transmuted her personal experiences and struggles into art, achieving critical acclaim and personal liberation.
translatedText: Ta sami muryarta ta hanyar rubuce-rubuce, ta yin amfani da sunan karya na namiji don ketare shingen al'umma. Ta canza abubuwan da ta samu na sirri da gwagwarmaya zuwa fasaha, tana samun babban yabo da 'yanci.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karni na 19

sourceText: Paul Cézanne
translatedText: Paul Cezanne

sourceText: Cézanne faced lifelong rejection from the mainstream art world, leading to profound isolation. His revolutionary style was difficult for his contemporaries to accept, and he endured critical indifference and public apathy, which caused considerable frustration. His father also strongly opposed his artistic ambitions, creating financial and emotional pressure.
translatedText: Cézanne ta fuskanci ƙi na tsawon rai daga duniyar fasaha na yau da kullun, wanda ya haifar da keɓantawa sosai. Salon juyin juya hali nasa ya yi wuya mutanen zamaninsa su yarda da shi, kuma ya jure rashin ko in kula da rashin jin dadin jama'a, wanda ya haifar da takaici. Mahaifinsa kuma ya yi adawa da burinsa na fasaha, yana haifar da matsin lamba na kudi da na zuciya.

sourceText: Despite these adversies, Cézanne remained steadfast in his artistic convictions. He immersed himself in his work in solitude, meticulously observing and analyzing nature to develop his unique visual language. He used failure and criticism as fuel for growth, persistently forging his own path. Ultimately, he was recognized as a master, greatly inspiring subsequent generations of artists.
translatedText: Duk da waɗannan matsalolin, Cézanne ya kasance da tsayin daka a cikin hukuncin fasaha. Ya nutsar da kansa cikin aikinsa cikin kadaici, yana lura da nazarin yanayi don bunkasa harshensa na musamman na gani. Ya yi amfani da gazawa da suka a matsayin makamashi don haɓaka, yana ci gaba da ƙirƙira hanyarsa. Daga ƙarshe, an gane shi a matsayin ƙwararren malami, wanda ya ƙarfafa tsararrun masu fasaha na gaba.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Marigayi 19th - Farkon ƙarni na 20

sourceText: Auguste Rodin
translatedText: Auguste Rodin

sourceText: Rodin faced significant setbacks early in his career, including failing the entrance examination for the École des Beaux-Arts three times. His unconventional style and focus on raw emotion often clashed with the academic traditions of the time, leading to criticism and rejection. Financial instability and the struggle for recognition were constant companions.
translatedText: Rodin ya fuskanci koma baya a farkon aikinsa, ciki har da kasa cin jarrabawar shiga École des Beaux-Arts sau uku. Salon sa wanda ba na al'ada ba da kuma mayar da hankali ga danyen motsin rai sau da yawa yakan ci karo da al'adun ilimi na lokacin, wanda ya haifar da suka da ƙin yarda. Rashin kwanciyar hankali na kudi da gwagwarmayar neman amincewa sun kasance abokai na dindindin.

sourceText: Despite these challenges, Rodin's unwavering dedication to his artistic vision fueled his perseverance. He relentlessly experimented with materials and techniques, developing a powerful new language of sculpture that conveyed psychological depth and physical vitality. As his work gained traction and admiration, he achieved international renown, cementing his legacy as a pioneer of modern sculpture.
translatedText: Duk da waɗannan ƙalubalen, sadaukarwar da Rodin ya yi ga hangen nesansa na fasaha ya ƙara ƙarfin juriyarsa. Ya yi gwajin kayan aiki da fasaha ba tare da ɓata lokaci ba, yana haɓaka sabon harshe mai ƙarfi na sassaka wanda ke isar da zurfin tunani da kuzarin jiki. Yayin da aikinsa ya samu karbuwa da sha'awa, ya samu suna a duniya, inda ya tabbatar da abin da ya bari a matsayinsa na farkon sassaka na zamani.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karni na 19

sourceText: Claude Debussy
translatedText: Claude Debussy

sourceText: He faced criticism from peers for his musical innovations and resistance from the traditional music establishment, alongside financial difficulties and chronic health issues.
translatedText: Ya fuskanci suka daga takwarorinsa saboda sabbin kade-kaden da ya ke yi da kuma tsayin daka daga kafa wakokin gargajiya, tare da matsalolin kudi da kuma matsalolin kiwon lafiya.

sourceText: By steadfastly adhering to his artistic vision, he forged a unique and original body of work. He drew inspiration from nature, poetry, and visual arts, overcoming hardships by transmuting them into music.
translatedText: Ta wurin tsayawa tsayin daka ga hangen nesansa na fasaha, ya ƙirƙira wani nau'in aiki na musamman da na asali. Ya zana wahayi daga yanayi, waƙa, da fasahar gani, yana shawo kan wahalhalu ta hanyar canza su zuwa kiɗa.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Marigayi 19th - Farkon ƙarni na 20

sourceText: Virginia Woolf
translatedText: Virginia Woolf

sourceText: The deaths of her mother and father in childhood, sexual abuse by her half-brother, and lifelong struggles with severe mental illness (bipolar disorder), leading to intense suffering and multiple suicide attempts.
translatedText: Mutuwar mahaifiyarta da mahaifinta a lokacin ƙuruciya, cin zarafin ɗan'uwanta na jima'i, da gwagwarmayar rayuwa ta rayuwa tare da matsanancin ciwon hauka (cututtukan bipolar), wanda ke haifar da wahala mai tsanani da yunƙurin kashe kansa.

sourceText: She sought to explore her inner world through writing, found intellectual companionship with her husband Leonard Woolf, and attempted to find mental stability through her association with the Bloomsbury Group. Through her literary achievements and life as a progressive thinker, she navigated her suffering and continued her artistic existence.
translatedText: Ta nemi bincika duniyarta ta cikin rubuce-rubuce, ta sami abokantaka na hankali tare da mijinta Leonard Woolf, kuma ta yi ƙoƙarin samun kwanciyar hankali ta hanyar haɗin gwiwarta da Rukunin Bloomsbury. Ta hanyar nasarorin da ta samu na adabi da rayuwa a matsayinta na mai tunani mai ci gaba, ta zagaya wahalarta ta ci gaba da kasancewarta na fasaha.

sourceText: Early 20th Century
translatedText: Farkon Karni na 20

sourceText: Franz Kafka
translatedText: Franz Kafka

sourceText: The pressure from his relationship with his father, the conflict between his daily job and literary aspirations, severe self-criticism and anxiety, and deteriorating health due to tuberculosis.
translatedText: Matsi daga dangantakarsa da mahaifinsa, da rikici tsakanin aikinsa na yau da kullum da burin adabi, tsananin zargi da damuwa, da tabarbarewar lafiya saboda tarin fuka.

sourceText: He asked his friend to burn his works before his death, but contrary to his will, his writings were published, earning him worldwide fame. His suffering and anxiety ironically became the source of his literary depth.
translatedText: Ya roki abokinsa da ya kona ayyukansa kafin mutuwarsa, amma sabanin yadda ya so, an buga rubuce-rubucensa, wanda ya sa ya shahara a duniya. Wahalarsa da damuwa sun zama tushen zurfin adabinsa.

sourceText: Early 20th Century
translatedText: Farkon Karni na 20

sourceText: James Joyce
translatedText: James Joyce

sourceText: Joyce suffered from extreme poverty, exile, constant family strife, and severe eyesight problems. His works often reflect these personal trials.
translatedText: Joyce ta sha fama da matsananciyar talauci, gudun hijira, rigingimun iyali da kuma matsalolin gani sosai. Ayyukansa sau da yawa suna nuna waɗannan gwaji na sirri.

sourceText: He overcame these difficulties through an unwavering dedication to his art and his experimental writing. His creative output while in exile further enriched his literary contributions.
translatedText: Ya shawo kan waɗannan matsalolin ta hanyar sadaukar da kai ga fasaha da rubuce-rubuce na gwaji. Ayyukansa na kirkire-kirkire yayin da yake gudun hijira ya kara wadatar da gudunmawarsa ta adabi.

sourceText: Early 20th Century
translatedText: Farkon Karni na 20

sourceText: Giacomo Puccini
translatedText: Giacomo Puccini

sourceText: In his later years, Puccini suffered immensely from lung cancer. He also endured significant emotional distress stemming from his complicated relationship with his wife, Elvira, and public criticism. Some of his works also faced critical backlash from traditional opera circles.
translatedText: A cikin shekarunsa na baya, Puccini ya sha wahala sosai daga ciwon huhu. Ya kuma jimre da baƙin ciki mai tsanani wanda ya samo asali daga rikitacciyar dangantakarsa da matarsa, Elvira, da sukar jama'a. Wasu daga cikin ayyukansa kuma sun fuskanci koma baya daga wasannin opera na gargajiya.

sourceText: Despite his suffering, Puccini remained passionately dedicated to his final opera, 'Turandot.' His immense talent and indomitable spirit ultimately ensured his works achieved immortality, forever resonating with opera lovers worldwide.
translatedText: Duk da wahalarsa, Puccini ya ci gaba da sadaukar da kai ga wasan opera na ƙarshe, 'Turandot.' Babban hazakarsa da ruhinsa mara ƙarfi daga ƙarshe sun tabbatar da ayyukansa sun kai ga dawwama, har abada suna jin daɗin masoyan opera a duk duniya.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Marigayi 19th - Farkon ƙarni na 20

sourceText: Charles Baudelaire
translatedText: Charles Baudelaire

sourceText: Estrangement from his stepfather, constant financial struggles, and chronic illness exacerbated by syphilis.
translatedText: Nisanta daga uban uban nasa, gwagwarmayar kuɗi akai-akai, da rashin lafiya mai tsanani wanda syphilis ya tsananta.

sourceText: He transmuted his painful reality into art, particularly in 'The Flowers of Evil,' striving to capture the complexity of the human psyche and the dual nature of urban beauty and squalor.
translatedText: Ya canza gaskiyarsa mai raɗaɗi zuwa fasaha, musamman a cikin 'Furen Mugunta', yana ƙoƙari ya kama sarƙaƙƙiyar ruhin ɗan adam da dabi'u biyu na kyawawan birane da ƙazanta.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karni na 19

sourceText: Wassily Kandinsky
translatedText: Wassily Kandinsky

sourceText: Initially a law professor, Kandinsky faced societal expectations and a lack of understanding from the established art world due to his deep passion for art. He also endured exile and political turmoil during turbulent times.
translatedText: Da farko farfesa na shari'a, Kandinsky ya fuskanci tsammanin al'umma da rashin fahimta daga duniyar fasaha da aka kafa saboda zurfin sha'awar fasaha. Ya kuma jure gudun hijira da rikicin siyasa a lokutan tashin hankali.

sourceText: He overcame these challenges through art, by listening to his inner voice. By purely expressing color and form, he built his unique artistic world and established theories, greatly influencing future generations.
translatedText: Ya shawo kan waɗannan ƙalubalen ta hanyar fasaha, ta hanyar sauraron muryarsa ta ciki. Ta hanyar bayyana launi da siffa kawai, ya gina duniyar fasaha ta musamman kuma ya kafa ka'idoji, yana tasiri sosai ga al'ummomi masu zuwa.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Marigayi 19th - Farkon ƙarni na 20

sourceText: Paul Gauguin
translatedText: Paul Gauguin

sourceText: Profound disillusionment with the materialism and philistinism of the Western world, estrangement from his family, poverty, illness, and artistic isolation.
translatedText: Tsananin rashin kunya game da son abin duniya da falsafancin yammacin duniya, nisantar danginsa, talauci, rashin lafiya, da keɓewar fasaha.

sourceText: He sought inspiration in the primitive, pure nature and human life, escaping from civilized society. Through the vibrant colors and life energy of Tahiti, he strove to create his own artistic language and find inner peace.
translatedText: Ya nemi wahayi a cikin tsattsauran yanayi, tsaftataccen yanayi da rayuwar ɗan adam, yana tserewa daga al'umma mai wayewa. Ta wurin launuka masu haske da kuzarin rayuwa na Tahiti, ya yi ƙoƙari ya ƙirƙiri yarensa na fasaha da samun kwanciyar hankali.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Marigayi 19th - Farkon ƙarni na 20

sourceText: William Blake
translatedText: William Blake

sourceText: Throughout his life, Blake's unique art and ideas were often misunderstood and criticized by his contemporaries. He faced financial hardship, social isolation, and periods of intense mental anguish.
translatedText: A cikin rayuwarsa, musamman fasahar fasaha da ra'ayoyin Blake galibi an yi musu rashin fahimta da suka daga mutanen zamaninsa. Ya fuskanci matsalar kuɗi, warewar jama'a, da lokutan baƙin ciki mai tsanani.

sourceText: He transcended these struggles through his profound inner vision and imaginative world. Blake developed his own methods of self-publication to share his art, and his work profoundly influenced subsequent generations.
translatedText: Ya wuce waɗannan gwagwarmaya ta hanyar hangen nesansa mai zurfi da duniyar tunaninsa. Blake ya haɓaka nasa hanyoyin buga kansa don raba fasahar sa, kuma aikinsa ya yi tasiri sosai ga tsararraki masu zuwa.

sourceText: Late 18th - Early 19th Century
translatedText: Marigayi 18th - Farkon Ƙarni na 19

sourceText: Hans Christian Andersen
translatedText: Hans Christian Andersen

sourceText: He endured a poverty-stricken and difficult childhood, constant self-doubt, societal prejudice, and the pain of unrequited love.
translatedText: Ya jure wa kuruciya ta kunci da wahala, da shakkun kai akai-akai, son zuciya, da radadin soyayyar da ba ta da tushe.

sourceText: Through writing, he transformed his suffering and wounds into art. His imagination and artistic talent opened doors to the world, and his works conveyed profound emotion rooted in these experiences.
translatedText: Ta hanyar rubuce-rubuce, ya canza wahalarsa da raunukansa zuwa fasaha. Hasashensa da basirarsa sun buɗe kofa ga duniya, kuma ayyukansa sun ba da zurfafa tunani a cikin waɗannan abubuwan.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karni na 19

sourceText: Rudyard Kipling
translatedText: Rudyard Kipling

sourceText: The abrupt separation from his parents in childhood, a harsh upbringing, and the early death of his daughter Josephine from a brain tumor brought him profound sorrow.
translatedText: Rabuwar ba zato da iyayensa a lokacin ƙuruciya, tarbiya mai tsauri, da farkon mutuwar ’yarsa Josephine daga ciwan ƙwaƙwalwa sun kawo masa baƙin ciki sosai.

sourceText: Kipling sublimated his pain through writing, capturing the complex realities of the Imperial age in his works and achieving literary success. He rediscovered life's meaning and overcame his suffering through his craft.
translatedText: Kipling ya ƙaddamar da ciwonsa ta hanyar rubuce-rubuce, yana kama abubuwan da suka faru na zamanin Imperial a cikin ayyukansa da kuma samun nasarar wallafe-wallafe. Ya sake gano ma'anar rayuwa kuma ya shawo kan wahalarsa ta hanyar fasaharsa.

sourceText: Late Victorian to Edwardian Era
translatedText: Late Victorian zuwa Edwardian Era

sourceText: Dorothea Lange
translatedText: Dorothea Lange

sourceText: Lange contracted polio as a child, leaving her with a permanent limp, a constant reminder of physical vulnerability. The Great Depression presented immense emotional and psychological strain as she documented widespread poverty and despair across America. Her personal life also faced significant challenges.
translatedText: Lange ta kamu da cutar shan inna tun tana karama, inda ya bar ta da rame na dindindin, abin tunasarwa akai-akai game da raunin jiki. Babban Bacin rai ya gabatar da matsananciyar damuwa da tunani yayin da ta rubuta talauci da rashin bege a fadin Amurka. Ita ma rayuwarta ta fuskanci ƙalubale masu yawa.

sourceText: Despite her physical challenges, Lange channeled her experiences into her art, becoming a powerful advocate for social justice through her photographs. She found strength in bearing witness to the suffering of others and used her camera as a tool to foster empathy and inspire change, ultimately transforming her personal pain into a force for public good.
translatedText: Duk da ƙalubalen da take fuskanta, Lange ta ba da labarin abubuwan da ta samu a cikin fasaharta, ta zama mai ba da shawara mai ƙarfi don tabbatar da adalci ta zamantakewa ta hanyar hotunanta. Ta sami ƙarfi wajen ba da shaida game da wahalar wasu kuma ta yi amfani da kyamararta a matsayin kayan aiki don haɓaka tausayawa da haɓaka canji, a ƙarshe ta mai da ciwon kanta zuwa wani ƙarfi don amfanin jama'a.

sourceText: 20th Century
translatedText: Karni na 20

sourceText: Clara Schumann
translatedText: Clara Schumann

sourceText: Clara faced immense challenges throughout her life. As a child prodigy, she endured rigorous training and demanding performance schedules dictated by her father. Later, she grappled with her husband Robert Schumann's debilitating mental illness, the ensuing financial instability, and the profound grief of losing children, all while raising eight children.
translatedText: Clara ta fuskanci ƙalubale masu yawa a duk rayuwarta. A matsayinta na ƙwararren yaro, ta jimre da ƙwaƙƙwaran horo da jadawali na aikin da mahaifinta ya faɗa. Daga baya, ta yi fama da rashin lafiya na tabin hankali maigidanta Robert Schumann, rashin kwanciyar hankali na kuɗi da ya biyo baya, da kuma baƙin cikin rashin ƴaƴa, duk sa'ad da suke renon yara takwas.

sourceText: Despite these profound hardships, Clara's resilience shone through. She continued her illustrious career as a concert pianist, becoming one of the most respected musicians of her time. After Robert's death, she dedicated herself to preserving and promoting his musical legacy. She also managed her household and nurtured her children, demonstrating remarkable strength and determination.
translatedText: Duk da waɗannan wahalhalu masu yawa, juriyar Clara ta haskaka sosai. Ta ci gaba da sana'arta mai ban sha'awa a matsayin mai wasan piano, ta zama ɗaya daga cikin mawakan da ake girmamawa a lokacinta. Bayan mutuwar Robert, ta sadaukar da kanta don kiyayewa da haɓaka gadonsa na kiɗa. Ta kuma kula da gidanta da renon 'ya'yanta, ta nuna karfi da jajircewa.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karni na 19

sourceText: Jane Addams
translatedText: Jane Addams

sourceText: Suffered from the early death of her mother, physical pain due to scoliosis, and societal prejudice. She also experienced mental anguish from the gap between idealism and reality.
translatedText: Ta sha wahala daga farkon mutuwar mahaifiyarta, ciwon jiki saboda scoliosis, da kuma son zuciya na al'umma. Ta kuma fuskanci bacin rai daga rata tsakanin manufa da gaskiya.

sourceText: Transformed her personal suffering into empathy for others. By founding Hull House, she provided education, culture, and welfare services to the poor, immigrants, women, and children, fostering social solidarity.
translatedText: Ta canza wahalhalunta zuwa tausayi ga wasu. Ta hanyar kafa Hull House, ta ba da ilimi, al'adu, da ayyukan jin daɗi ga matalauta, baƙi, mata, da yara, haɓaka haɗin kai na zamantakewa.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Marigayi 19th - Farkon ƙarni na 20

sourceText: Maria Montessori
translatedText: Maria Montessori

sourceText: Societal prejudice and discrimination faced as a woman pursuing medicine and a career, along with the challenges of establishing her educational method.
translatedText: Wariya da wariya na al'umma da ake fuskanta a matsayin mace mai neman aikin likita da sana'a, tare da kalubalen kafa tsarin karatun ta.

sourceText: Through sheer determination, persistent research, and a deep love for children, she overcame these barriers to become a globally recognized educator.
translatedText: Ta hanyar jajircewa, bincike mai tsayi, da soyayya mai zurfi ga yara, ta shawo kan waɗannan shingen don zama sanannen malami a duniya.

sourceText: Late 19th - Mid 20th Century
translatedText: Marigayi 19th - Tsakiyar Karni na 20

sourceText: William Lloyd Garrison
translatedText: William Lloyd Garrison

sourceText: Garrison faced immense opposition, including threats of violence, mob attacks, legal persecution, and social ostracism for his unwavering stance against slavery. His radical calls for immediate abolition often put him at odds with more moderate reformers.
translatedText: Garrison ya fuskanci adawa mai yawa, gami da barazanar tashin hankali, hare-haren ’yan tawaye, zalunci na shari’a, da kuma kyamar jama’a saboda matsayarsa da ba ta da tushe balle makama. Kiraye-kirayensa masu tsattsauran ra'ayi na a kawar da shi cikin gaggawa yakan sanya shi cikin sabani da karin masu sassaucin ra'ayi.

sourceText: He persevered through sheer determination, the unwavering support of fellow abolitionists, and the powerful platform of his newspaper, 'The Liberator.' His relentless advocacy, despite personal hardship and public condemnation, contributed significantly to the eventual passage of the 13th Amendment.
translatedText: Ya jajirce ta hanyar azama, da goyon bayan 'yan'uwantaka na kawar da kai, da kuma dandamali mai karfi na jaridarsa, 'The Liberator'. Shawarar sa na rashin jajircewa, duk da wahalhalun da jama'a ke fuskanta, sun ba da gudummawa sosai ga wucewar gyare-gyare na 13.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karni na 19

sourceText: Albert Schweitzer
translatedText: Albert Schweitzer ne adam wata

sourceText: Lack of medical knowledge, primitive medical conditions, diseases like malaria, racial prejudice, and the hardships of war.
translatedText: Rashin ilimin likitanci, yanayin likita na farko, cututtuka kamar zazzabin cizon sauro, wariyar launin fata, da wahalhalun yaƙi.

sourceText: Through continuous study and research, developing treatments suitable for local conditions, and a spirit of dedicated service, he overcame these challenges and earned the trust of the African people.
translatedText: Ta hanyar ci gaba da karatu da bincike, haɓaka jiyya da suka dace da yanayin gida, da ruhin hidimar sadaukarwa, ya shawo kan waɗannan ƙalubalen kuma ya sami amincewar mutanen Afirka.

sourceText: Late 19th Century - Mid 20th Century
translatedText: Marigayi Ƙarni na 19 - Tsakiyar Ƙarni na 20

sourceText: Pandita Ramabai
translatedText: Rev. Ramabai

sourceText: Societal prejudice faced as a woman, the custom of child marriage, the death of her husband, and isolation due to social stigma.
translatedText: Ra’ayin al’umma da ake fuskanta a matsayin mace, da al’adar auren ‘ya’ya, mutuwar mijinta, da kebewa saboda kyamar zamantakewa.

sourceText: She overcame these barriers through her knowledge and education. She established schools and shelters for women and created opportunities for economic independence for widows and remarried women.
translatedText: Ta shawo kan waɗannan shinge ta hanyar iliminta da iliminta. Ta kafa makarantu da matsuguni ga mata tare da samar da damammaki na yancin tattalin arziki ga gwauraye da matan da suka sake aure.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karni na 19

sourceText: Elizabeth Blackwell
translatedText: Elizabeth Blackwell

sourceText: Faced immense discrimination and rejection from medical schools solely because of her gender. She endured social ostracism and skepticism from the male-dominated medical establishment.
translatedText: Ta fuskanci tsananin wariya da ƙin yarda daga makarantun likitanci kawai saboda jinsinta. Ta jure wa al'umma kyama da shakku daga rukunin likitocin da maza suka mamaye.

sourceText: Through unwavering determination and resilience, she graduated and went on to establish the New York Infirmary for Indigent Women and Children, providing crucial medical care and training opportunities for women.
translatedText: Ta hanyar jajircewa da tsayin daka, ta kammala karatun digiri kuma ta ci gaba da kafa Cibiyar Kula da Lafiya ta New York don Mata da Yara marasa galihu, tana ba da mahimmancin kulawar likita da horo ga mata.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karni na 19

sourceText: Florence Kelley
translatedText: Florence Kelly ne adam wata

sourceText: Despite a privileged upbringing, she grappled with profound concerns about social justice and the limitations imposed on women. Witnessing the suffering of those in harsh labor conditions filled her with deep sorrow and indignation.
translatedText: Duk da kasancewarta gata da ta samu, ta kokawa da tsananin damuwa game da adalci da kuma gazawar da aka sanya mata. Shaidar wahalar waɗanda suke cikin mawuyacin hali ya sa ta baƙin ciki sosai da fushi.

sourceText: By pursuing legal studies, she gained the knowledge to champion women's rights. Collaborating with various social reform organizations, she spearheaded efforts to enact legislation for child labor prohibition, minimum wages, and reduced working hours for women. Her unyielding determination and perseverance drove significant social change.
translatedText: Ta hanyar karatun shari'a, ta sami ilimin don kare hakkin mata. Ta hada kai da kungiyoyi daban-daban na kawo sauyi a zamantakewa, ta jagoranci yunkurin kafa dokar hana yiwa yara aiki aiki, mafi karancin albashi, da rage lokutan aiki ga mata. Jajircewarta da jajircewarta ta haifar da gagarumin sauyi na zamantakewa.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Marigayi 19th - Farkon ƙarni na 20

sourceText: David Livingstone
translatedText: David Livingstone

sourceText: Severe illness, isolation, the suffering of indigenous Africans, the brutality of the slave trade, the hardships and dangers of exploration.
translatedText: Mummunan rashin lafiya, keɓewa, wahalar da ƴan asalin Afirka ke fama da su, muguwar cinikin bayi, wahalhalu da haɗarin bincike.

sourceText: Overcame challenges through strong faith, an inquisitive spirit, humanitarianism, persistent effort, and a deep affection for Africa and its people.
translatedText: An shawo kan ƙalubale ta wurin bangaskiya mai ƙarfi, ruhin bincike, jin daɗin jin kai, ƙoƙari mai tsayi, da zurfin ƙauna ga Afirka da mutanenta.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karni na 19

sourceText: Alfred Nobel
translatedText: Alfred Nobel

sourceText: Throughout my life, I was consumed by invention. However, witnessing my creation, dynamite, being used for war and destruction caused me profound anguish. The knowledge that my innovations could harm humanity was a great burden.
translatedText: A tsawon rayuwata, ƙirƙira ta cinye ni. Duk da haka, ganin yadda aka halicce ni, mai ƙarfi, ana amfani da shi don yaƙi da halaka ya sa ni baƙin ciki ƙwarai. Sanin cewa sabbin abubuwa na za su iya cutar da bil'adama babban nauyi ne.

sourceText: Amidst this distress, I resolved to dedicate my fortune to rewarding those who advance human progress and peace. Through the Nobel Prizes, I aimed to counterbalance the potential negative impacts of my work and foster a brighter future.
translatedText: A cikin wannan baƙin ciki, na ƙudiri aniyar sadaukar da dukiyata don ba wa waɗanda suke ciyar da ci gaban ɗan adam da zaman lafiya. Ta hanyar lambobin yabo na Nobel, na yi niyyar daidaita ma'auni mara kyau na aikina da haɓaka kyakkyawar makoma.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karni na 19

sourceText: Pierre de Coubertin
translatedText: Pierre de Coubertin

sourceText: Following France's defeat in the Franco-Prussian War, he felt a profound need for national pride restoration and educational reform. His idea for reviving the Olympics initially faced skepticism and financial difficulties.
translatedText: Bayan kashin da Faransa ta sha a yakin Franco-Prussian, ya ji matukar bukatar maido da martabar kasa da kuma gyara ilimi. Tunaninsa na farfado da gasar Olympics da farko ya fuskanci shakku da matsalolin kudi.

sourceText: Coubertin overcame these challenges with a fervent desire to revive the spirit of the ancient Olympic Games in the modern era. He believed that international sporting events could foster understanding and peace among nations, and he tirelessly persuaded and fundraised for this cause.
translatedText: Coubertin ya shawo kan waɗannan ƙalubalen tare da ƙwaƙƙwaran sha'awar farfaɗo da ruhin tsohon wasannin Olympics a wannan zamani. Ya yi imanin cewa al'amuran wasanni na kasa da kasa za su iya samar da fahimta da zaman lafiya a tsakanin al'ummomi, kuma ya jajirce tare da tattara kudade don wannan dalili.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Marigayi 19th - Farkon ƙarni na 20

sourceText: Fridtjof Nansen
translatedText: Fridtjof Nansen

sourceText: The extreme isolation and harsh conditions of Arctic exploration, the immense suffering of refugees following World War I, and the often indifferent response from the international community.
translatedText: Matsananciyar warewa da yanayi mai tsauri na binciken Arctic, da tsananin wahalar da 'yan gudun hijira suka fuskanta bayan yakin duniya na daya, da kuma martanin rashin ko in kula daga kasashen duniya.

sourceText: His indomitable spirit, scientific rigor, and deep empathy allowed him to overcome physical and political challenges, ultimately leading to significant humanitarian aid and diplomatic achievements.
translatedText: Ruhinsa marar karewa, tsananin kimiyya, da zurfin tausayi ya ba shi damar shawo kan kalubale na zahiri da na siyasa, wanda hakan ya haifar da gagarumin taimakon jin kai da nasarorin diflomasiya.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Marigayi 19th - Farkon ƙarni na 20

sourceText: George Eastman
translatedText: George Eastman
